The slots were from South American rainforests, and perished in storage warehouses between December 2024…

Mills, who is running for the U.S. Senate, wrote in a letter Friday to the…

items.title: This 100 million-year-old snake had hind legs and a lost bone that changes evolution
items.summary: Nearly 100 million years ago, snakes weren’t the sleek, limbless creatures we know today—they still had hind legs and even a cheekbone that has almost vanished in modern species. A remarkably preserved fossil of Najash rionegrina from Argentina has reshaped how scientists think about snake origins, suggesting early snakes were large, wide-mouthed predators rather than tiny burrowers.

items.title: Scientists just uncovered a 3 million-year climate mystery in Antarctic ice
items.summary: Ancient Antarctic ice is revealing a surprising new chapter in Earth’s climate story, stretching back 3 million years. By analyzing tiny pockets of trapped air and rare gases, scientists have discovered that while the planet cooled significantly—especially in the oceans—levels of key greenhouse gases like carbon dioxide and methane changed only modestly. This unexpected mismatch suggests other powerful forces, such as shifting ice sheets, ocean circulation, and Earth’s reflectivity, played major roles in driving long-term climate change.

items.title: Stunning 132 million-year-old dinosaur tracks are rewriting history
items.summary: A long-standing mystery in southern Africa’s fossil record is beginning to unravel. After massive lava flows 182 million years ago seemed to erase evidence of dinosaurs in the region, scientists have now uncovered surprising new clues along the Western Cape coast. Dozens of dinosaur tracks, about 132 million years old, have been discovered in a tiny stretch of rock near Knysna—making them the youngest ever found in southern Africa.

items.title: The earliest evidence of the first stars may lie in a distant gas clump
items.summary: James Webb data reveal pristine gas irradiated by energetic light some 450 million years after the Big Bang — a sign it may house primordial stars.

items.title: Ancient DNA tests the notion that allergies are due to our dirtier past
items.summary: An analysis of ancient DNA and modern disease risk suggests some immune genes may reduce allergy risk rather than increase it.

items.title: The secret to perfect espresso? It’s physics
items.summary: Inspired by gas and liquid flow in earth science, researchers brewed an equation to calculate the speed of water percolation through ground coffee.
